signals.headline: BANK OF KOREA HOLDS RATE STEADY AMID MIDDLE EAST OIL SHOCK | NO | HIGH CONFIDENCE**
signals.raw:
BANK OF KOREA HOLDS RATE STEADY AMID MIDDLE EAST OIL SHOCK | NO | HIGH CONFIDENCE**

The **Bank of Korea kept its benchmark interest rate at 2.5% for a seventh consecutive month** as of its April 10, 2026 monetary policy decision, maintaining its hawkish stance despite moderating headline inflation.[3] This was Governor Rhee Chang-yong's final rate-setting meeting before stepping down.[3]
